title: Cinematic FPV Freestyle & Flow by Le Drib
creator: Le Drib
award_or_showcase_provenance: Not a juried-contest or festival selection for this specific video. Independently corroborated this session via two sources: (1) the video's own extracted description text, which discloses a full sponsored gear stack including 'Hypetrain Le Drib Motor V2', a 'Skyeliner' airframe co-branded with the creator's own name, and 'Rates & PIDs: rotorriot.com/products/le-dribs-tune-and-rates' -- i.e. Le Drib has a namesake signature product line sold through Rotor Riot, not merely an affiliate-link mention; (2) an independent WebSearch this session that found a GetFPV.com product-review page ('Rotor Riot Hypetrain – Le Drib 2306 2650kv Motor') and a standalone rotorriot.com/collections/le-drib storefront page, both corroborating the same namesake motor/gear line from a source other than the video itself. This is a genuine, disclosed sponsorship/reputation signal (a commercially sold, name-branded product line implies a piloting style/reputation the sponsor judged worth commercializing), but it is a product-endorsement signal, not a specific award, festival placement, or juried selection for this particular upload -- none was found or is claimed.
notes: CANDIDATE-SOURCED, INDEPENDENTLY RE-VERIFIED THIS SESSION -- one exemplar added to this pack's FPV-freestyle/proximity-flying subgenre bucket, distinct from this pack's existing single continuous-take FPV clips (e.g. flaviufpv-madeira-fanal-forest-fpv.json, drone-racing-league-waterfall-fpv-dive.json) and closer in kind to its other named-pilot showreel exemplars (captainvanover-fpv-cinematic-reel.json, johnnyfpv-miami-freestyle-fpv.json). The candidate (title 'Cinematic FPV Freestyle & Flow by Le Drib', creator 'Le Drib', source_url https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_QNPzX5a0kQ) was NOT trusted verbatim -- it was independently re-verified this session via YouTube's oEmbed endpoint (https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_QNPzX5a0kQ&format=json), which returned title='Cinematic FPV Freestyle & Flow by Le Drib', author_name='Le Drib', author_url='https://www.youtube.com/@LeDribFPV', provider_name='YouTube', embed height=113/width=200 -- an exact field-for-field match to the candidate's title/creator/source_url claims, and a landscape 16:9 aspect ratio, not a portrait/vertical embed. A second, independent verification step this session used curl with a desktop User-Agent to fetch the raw watch-page HTML directly (~1.25MB of markup; WebFetch's own summarizer only surfaced YouTube's generic footer/legal boilerplate for this page, so a direct fetch was used to reach the underlying player-response JSON instead) and extracted, via grep against the raw markup: lengthSeconds='240'/approxDurationMs='240039' (duration is set to 240 below -- a verified, non-null figure, not left null, because this session's own fetch actually surfaced the player-response JSON, unlike some other exemplars in this pack where that extraction failed), publishDate/uploadDate='2019-07-30T14:57:44-07:00', viewCount='132124', and the full shortDescription text. That shortDescription text reads (in relevant part): "A 'super edit' of some of the most cinematic moments during my FPV Freestyle adventures... With Love, Drew... Music by San Holo... Instagram: @drewcamden / Twitter: @drewcamden" followed by a disclosed gear list (GoPro HERO7 Black camera, Skyeliner Airframe, Hypetrain Le Drib Motor V2, DALRC F405 AIO flight controller, Rotor Riot Micro Eagle Pro JB Edition FPV camera, Fatshark HDO goggles). This independently corroborates several distinct facts, not merely the candidate's own framing: the creator's real name (Drew Camden), the video's own self-description as a 'super edit' (i.e. an edit-forward compilation assembled from multiple freestyle clips, not one continuous take), the named soundtrack artist (San Holo, a real, independently-known electronic musician), and a disclosed camera/FPV-cam hardware stack. A supplementary WebSearch this session ('Le Drib FPV pilot Rotor Riot French freestyle') corroborated, from sources independent of the video itself (GetFPV.com's product-review page for the 'Rotor Riot Hypetrain – Le Drib' motor, and rotorriot.com/collections/le-drib), that Le Drib is a real, publicly documented sponsored Rotor Riot pilot with his own namesake product line -- consistent with, and reinforcing, the video description's own gear-sponsorship claims rather than merely repeating them. `license_category` is set to the conservative default 'all-rights-reserved': the same raw-HTML fetch used for duration extraction was also grepped (case-insensitive) for 'creative commons', 'licensedForReuse', 'cc-by', and 'attribution license', with zero matches found anywhere in the page -- per this project's stated default (assume all-rights-reserved unless the uploader's license setting is directly confirmed otherwise). Consequently `local_media_path` stays null and no video file was downloaded or persisted anywhere in this repo, consistent with the spec's metadata-first default and its explicit prohibition on bulk-downloading all-rights-reserved footage; `flagged_for_user_signoff` does not apply here since the license-verified-but-withheld branch (constraint 3's cc-by/cc0/public-domain/owned case) never triggered -- this record defaulted to all-rights-reserved, the standard non-signoff path. `platform` is confirmed 'youtube' (re-verified, not carried over from the discovery-stage claim): the source_url is a standard youtube.com/watch URL and the oEmbed response's provider_name field independently returned 'YouTube'; this is not an Instagram URL, so the Instagram-specific platform='other'/local_media_path=null-forever rule does not apply (though local_media_path is still null under the separate all-rights-reserved rule above), and `is_instagram=false` from the discovery stage is confirmed correct. Note on the discovery-stage 'short-form/reel-style' framing supplied for this candidate ('assembled as quick, beat-matched sequences... widely clipped into shorter reposts by FPV fan/compilation accounts'): per this pack's own established precedent (see johnnyfpv-miami-freestyle-fpv.json's notes), a claim that OTHER accounts have re-clipped or reposted fragments of a creator's work is not, by itself, structural evidence about the canonical upload's own format -- it describes downstream repackaging, not this video. This record does not rely on that claim; instead, `editorial_style.format` is grounded directly in this session's own verified evidence (see editorial_style.pacing_notes below) -- specifically the video's own self-description as a 'super edit' compilation, which independently corroborates (rather than merely restates) the same underlying premise the discovery flag pointed at. `scores` (sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness) are `scores_provenance: manually_estimated` judgments, NOT measurements from Capability 1's actual scoring functions (scoring_sharpness.py/scoring_exposure.py/scoring_motion_smoothness.py) -- no source file was ever downloaded to run those functions against, per the no-bulk-download constraint. Calibration reasoning: sharpness=0.78 matches johnnyfpv-miami-freestyle-fpv.json's 0.78, on a comparable disclosed-hardware basis -- this video's description confirms the same primary camera (GoPro HERO7 Black) plus a disclosed dedicated analog FPV camera (Rotor Riot Micro Eagle Pro JB Edition), a similar 2018-2019-era hardware tier to johnnyfpv's GoPro Hero7 Black + RunCam Swift Mini 2 combination, and below this pack's top tier (captainvanover-fpv-cinematic-reel.json's 0.80, built on a professional Red Komodo RAW cinema camera). exposure=0.75 is set slightly below johnnyfpv's 0.77: no explicit golden-hour/HDR/log-profile framing was found for this video (unlike johnnyfpv's AirVuz-sourced 'golden hour paradise' description), so it sits at this pack's mid-band rather than its upper-mid tier. motion_smoothness=0.80 matches drone-racing-league-waterfall-fpv-dive.json's 0.80 and sits below captainvanover's 0.83 and johnnyfpv's 0.84: the disclosed Rotor Riot sponsorship and namesake signature motor/tune/airframe product line is a genuine, disclosed piloting-reputation signal (a sponsor selling gear under this pilot's own name implies a recognized flying style), but it is a narrower, product-endorsement-level signal, not the independently corroborated superlative-tier reputation this pack documents for captainvanover (2019 DRL World Champion, Hollywood film credits) or johnnyfpv (AirVuz staff description as 'the almost undisputed master of cinematic FPV flight', original 2015 DRL competitor) -- so it is scored a half-step below both rather than at their level. `composite_score` IS a real computation, not hand-computed: it was produced this session by calling this repo's own `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.composite.compute_composite_score` with these three manually-estimated inputs (sharpness=0.78, exposure=0.75, motion_smoothness=0.80, composition=None) and `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.weights.default_weights(weights_version='default-v1')` (equal 1/3 weighting on sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness, composition weight fixed at 0.0) via `.venv/bin/python -c "from drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.composite import compute_composite_score; from drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.weights import default_weights; w = default_weights(weights_version='default-v1'); print(compute_composite_score(0.78, 0.75, 0.80, None, w))"` in this session, which printed weights=ScoringWeights(weights_version='default-v1', sharpness=0.3333333333333333, exposure=0.3333333333333333, motion_smoothness=0.3333333333333333, composition=0.0) and composite_score=0.7766666666666666 -- because composition is None, compute_composite_score's defensive None-skipping behavior excludes it from both the weighted sum and the renormalization denominator, reducing the result to the equal-weighted average of the three present signals: (0.78+0.75+0.80)/3 = 0.7766666666666666 (rounded to 0.7767 here), confirmed by actually running the function this session rather than hand-computed.

editorial_style.pacing_notes: format='short-form-reel' is grounded in this video's own extracted description text, not a repetition of the candidate brief's discovery-stage framing: the creator's own shortDescription (extracted via this session's raw-HTML fetch) opens with 'A \"super edit\" of some of the most cinematic moments during my FPV Freestyle adventures', explicitly self-describing the video as an edit-forward compilation assembled from many separate freestyle clips rather than one continuous take, and separately credits a named musical soundtrack ('Music by San Holo'), consistent with genre-typical beat-matched cutting. This matches this pack's established 'short-form-reel' definition used elsewhere for similarly edit-forward, multi-clip, music-cut pieces (see captainvanover-fpv-cinematic-reel.json's parallel 'reel'/montage self-description) as distinct from a single unbroken cinematic take. This classification is explicitly NOT based on runtime or hosting structure: this session's own verification found duration=240s (4:00, well beyond a platform Short) and a standard landscape 16:9 embed (oEmbed height=113/width=200) on a regular /watch URL, not a /shorts/ or vertical-aspect delivery format -- so 'short-form-reel' here describes the multi-clip montage editing convention (per this pack's own precedent), not the YouTube Shorts product; this mirrors captainvanover-fpv-cinematic-reel.json's identical reasoning at a comparable 120s runtime. This record deliberately does NOT lean on the discovery brief's own claim that other accounts have 're-clipped'/'reposted' this creator's work into shorter pieces elsewhere -- per this pack's precedent set in johnnyfpv-miami-freestyle-fpv.json, a downstream repost claim about OTHER accounts' behavior is not evidence about this canonical video's own structure, and no independent WebSearch evidence of specific third-party re-clips was found or relied on in this session; the classification instead rests solely on this video's own verified self-description as a 'super edit'. No live playback or scrubbing of the video was attempted in this session (browser automation tools were available but not invoked, consistent with this pack's existing precedent for reel-style exemplars of not exercising an interactive browser session for a single-record bounded verification task), so estimated_cut_count, avg_shot_length_seconds, and transition_styles_observed stay null/empty rather than guessed -- no oEmbed, curl/raw-HTML, or WebSearch result in this session described this specific video's exact cut count, individual shot durations, or specific transition techniques (whip pans, hard cuts, speed ramps, etc.). review_method is set to 'text_provenance_only' to reflect that every editorial-style conclusion here comes from the video's own extracted description text and oEmbed/raw-HTML structural metadata, not from actually watching the video.
